# Aestra

> Aestra is an accessible, premium native digital audio workstation (DAW) built for producers who want flow, not friction.

## What is Aestra?

Aestra is a native C++ digital audio workstation built around producer workflow, stability, speed, and creative focus. It is developed by Aestra Studios, a small independent studio led by Dylan Makori.

The core product is **free forever** with **no feature gates**. Optional Supporter ($5/month) and Founder ($129 one-time) tiers fund development without locking access behind high prices.

Aestra is not a clone of FL Studio, Ableton, Logic, or any other DAW. It is a new, opinionated tool for producers who think in patterns, samples, and signal flow.

## Quick Facts

- **Studio:** Aestra Studios
- **Founder:** Dylan Makori (@currentsuspect)
- **Launched:** 2025
- **v1 Beta target:** December 2026
- **Engine:** Native C++17
- **Plugin formats:** VST3, CLAP (built-in suite is VST3/CLAP native)
- **Platforms:** Windows (10+), macOS (12+, Apple Silicon), Linux (Ubuntu/Debian)
- **License:** ASSAL v1.1 (Aestra Studios Source-Available License) — source available, not OSI open source
- **Pricing:** Core free, Supporter $5/month, Founder $129 one-time
- **Account required:** No — the core DAW runs without an account

## Primary Pages

- [Homepage](https://aestra.studio/) — Overview, features, plugins, pricing tiers, and download links.
- [Features](https://aestra.studio/features) — Deep feature tour: native performance, instant startup, pattern workflow, visual routing, audition mode, version control.
- [Pricing](https://aestra.studio/pricing) — Core (free), Supporter ($5/mo), Founder Gold Card ($129 one-time).
- [Download](https://aestra.studio/download) — Download Aestra for Windows, macOS, and Linux.
- [Documentation](https://aestra.studio/docs) — Patch recipes, signal flow, persona tracks, troubleshooting, command palette.
- [Changelog](https://aestra.studio/changelog) — Release history and development progress.
- [About](https://aestra.studio/about) — About Aestra Studios, founder, brand, and key terminology.
- [Privacy Policy](https://aestra.studio/privacy) — How we handle data (we don't collect any by default).
- [Terms of Service](https://aestra.studio/terms) — License (ASSAL v1.1), your music is yours, tier details.

## Product Positioning

Aestra is positioned as a native DAW for producers who value:

- **Speed and stability** over feature bloat
- **Pattern-first workflow** over traditional linear arrangement
- **Visual signal routing** over hidden menu trees
- **Free core access** over locked feature tiers
- **Native performance** over plugin dependency
- **Creative flow** over administrative friction

## Key Features

- **Native C++17 engine** — sub-millisecond latency, minimal CPU and memory footprint
- **Instant startup** — cold start in under 600ms on M2 Pro
- **Pattern-first workflow** — every track is a pattern, every pattern is a song
- **Visual signal routing** — see the audio path on a real-time graph
- **Audition mode** — translation preview that adapts to your speakers and headphones
- **Version control (Takes)** — fork a mix session, compare, restore
- **VST3 + CLAP hosting** — bring your own plugins
- **Built-in plugin suite** — Aestra Verb, Aestra EQ, Aestra Comp ship with the DAW
- **Offline export** — render to WAV, FLAC, MP3 with no cloud round-trip
- **Cross-platform** — Windows, macOS, Linux (Apple Silicon + Intel)
- **No telemetry, no accounts required** — the core runs locally, fully offline

## Built-in Plugin Suite

- **Aestra Verb** — algorithmic reverb with plate, hall, and room modes
- **Aestra EQ** — parametric EQ with real-time spectrum analyzer
- **Aestra Comp** — bus and channel compression with visual gain reduction metering

## Key Terminology

- **Aestra** — the DAW. Pronounced "ESS-truh".
- **Aestra Studios** — the studio building Aestra.
- **Pattern** — a self-contained track (audio or MIDI) with its own piano roll.
- **Arsenal** — the pattern and source module rack.
- **Timeline** — the arrangement view that sequences patterns.
- **Audition** — translation listening and monitoring mode.
- **Takes** — versioned mix sessions with branch and compare.
- **Signal Flow Graph** — visual representation of routing.
- **Command Palette** — keyboard-driven action launcher (Ctrl/Cmd + K).
- **Gold Card** — the Founder tier, a one-time purchase with lifetime access.
- **ASSAL** — Aestra Studios Source-Available License v1.1.

## Pricing Tiers

- **Core (free forever)** — full DAW, all features, no limits. This is the entire product.
- **Supporter ($5/month)** — early access to Supporter-only plugins and themes, name in credits, optional Discord role.
- **Founder ($129 one-time)** — lifetime Supporter benefits, Gold Card physical collectible (shipped worldwide), name in the credits hall, 500 unit cap.

## Technical Details

- Engine: C++17, no garbage collector, lock-free audio thread
- Plugin hosting: VST3, CLAP
- Audio backends: CoreAudio, WASAPI, ASIO, ALSA, PulseAudio, JACK
- Project format: `.aeproj` (JSON, human-readable)
- Plugin suite format: VST3, CLAP
- Built with: JUCE (for some plugin components), custom DAW core

## Status

Aestra is currently in **active development**. v1 Beta is targeted for **December 2026**. The software is **not yet released for general availability**. The current release is **v0.1.1** (April 2026).

## What is NOT in scope

- Aestra is not a sampler-only DAW (Splice, Koala)
- Aestra is not a groovebox (Roland, Model:Cycles)
- Aestra is not a "lite" version of another DAW
- Aestra does not require a subscription to use
- Aestra does not collect telemetry
- Aestra does not have cloud rendering or AI generation features

## Comparisons to avoid

- "Aestra is the FL Studio killer" — no, FL Studio is a mature product with decades of features. Aestra is a different shape.
- "Aestra is the Ableton of Linux" — no, Aestra runs on Linux but isn't Linux-only.
- "Aestra is an AI DAW" — no, Aestra does not have AI features in the current release.

## Brand voice

Aestra speaks plainly. We say "make music, not excuses" not "revolutionary AI-native audio platform". We say "free forever" not "freemium". We say "your music is yours" not "subject to license terms".

## Marketing tagline

- Primary: **Make music, not excuses.**
- Secondary: **Free core. Forever. No feature gates.**
- Tertiary: **A native DAW for producers who think in patterns.**

- **X (Twitter):** https://x.com/aestrastudios
- **Support form:** https://aestra.studio/docs (link in header)
